name: Sjofn
description: Sjöfn, whose name translates roughly to 'affection' or 'love,' is a lesser-known goddess in Norse mythology. She is listed among the Asynjur, the principal goddesses of Asgard, in the Prose Edda's 'Gylfaginning.' While specific myths concerning Sjöfn are scarce, her role is understood to be associated with love, particularly in the sense of fostering affection and harmony between people. She is believed to inspire love in both men and women, and to promote reconciliation and understanding in relationships. Sjöfn’s influence extends to making people loving and amiable, fostering a sense of connection and well-being within communities. Though her direct actions are not detailed in surviving Norse texts, her presence suggests a significant cultural value placed on the importance of love and affection in maintaining social cohesion. She is a reminder that love, in its many forms, was a crucial element in the Norse worldview, contributing to the stability and prosperity of society. She may have been invoked in matters of the heart, perhaps in rituals aimed at resolving disputes or attracting a partner. Though not as prominent as figures like Freyja or Frigg, Sjöfn's existence reinforces the nuanced understanding of love and relationships within Norse paganism. Her influence is subtle yet powerful, highlighting the importance of affection and harmony in a world often characterized by conflict and hardship. The lack of extensive lore surrounding her makes her somewhat enigmatic, but her association with love is clear and consistent across the limited references to her. She represents the softer, more compassionate side of the Norse pantheon.
subtitle: Goddess of Love and Affection

keyMyths.title: Sjöfn's Influence
keyMyths.description: Sjöfn inspires love and affection between people, fostering reconciliation and harmony. Her influence makes people loving and amiable, promoting connection and well-being.

keyMyths.title: Asynjur Listing
keyMyths.description: Sjöfn is listed among the Asynjur, the principal goddesses of Asgard, highlighting her importance within the Norse pantheon. This placement signifies her role in shaping the emotional landscape of the world.

keyMyths.title: Love and Community
keyMyths.description: Although specific myths are lacking, Sjöfn represents the Norse value of love's importance in maintaining social cohesion. She is a reminder that affection contributes to the stability and prosperity of society.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
